page: The UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server configured with 48 vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration features 48 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core and shared CPU allocation. The system includes 2048 GB of local SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate a clear contrast between single-core and multi-threaded performance. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the bottom 10% and single-threaded gzip compression in the bottom 25%. However, multi-threaded capabilities are robust, as demonstrated by a strong PassMark CPU score and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the middle 50% tier, with read-only large block speeds at 112.34 GB/sec and write speeds at 64.71 GB/sec.

Given the high core count and substantial memory capacity, this server provides a dense resource profile suitable for parallelized workloads. It is well-suited for multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and database operations, where the average PassMark database score of 16084.01 and Redis SET performance of 42.02 million operations per second are highly relevant. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, predictable single-core performance, but the high resource density offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, non-continuous tasks.
description: The UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a KVM-based general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 vCPUs with shared allocation, 256 GB of RAM, and 2048 GB of local SSD storage. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-threaded performance is strong, highlighted by top-tier PassMark CPU and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform consistently in the average range. This resource-dense configuration is best suited for highly parallelized workloads, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and database operations that can leverage the high core count, while avoiding tasks heavily dependent on sustained single-core CPU performance.
og_description: UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2048 GB SSD storage, offering strong multi-threaded performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is an x86_64 virtual server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2048 GB SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core-count virtual server balancing 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB RAM with bundled 2 TB SSD storage for parallel processing.
bullet_points: 48 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
256 GB RAM and 2048 GB bundled local SSD storage
Strong multi-threaded performance and bzip3 decompression
Shared CPU allocation with lower single-core performance tradeoffs
Suited for parallel data processing, software compilation, and databases
